news.headline: United, free and brilliant - can anyone stop France masterclass?
news.desc: France boss Didier Deschamps urges caution despite watching his side dismantle Sweden to leave Les Bleus fans dreaming of winning the World Cup.

news.headline: Defying logic - how Haaland could end up with 260 international goals
news.desc: Erling Haaland delivers on the biggest stage yet for Norway - but just how many goals could he end up with in international football?

news.headline: Koeman resigns, while Netherlands report racist abuse
news.desc: Ronald Koeman resigns as Netherlands manager after their World Cup exit, which led to "appalling" racist abuse aimed at players who missed penalties.

news.headline: England right-backs Quansah and James out of DR Congo game
news.desc: Right-backs Jarell Quansah and Reece James will miss England's World Cup last-32 match against DR Congo because of injury.

news.headline: Tuchel will stick to Southgate's penalty plan
news.desc: England manager Thomas Tuchel had revealed that his team will follow his predecessor Sir Gareth Southgate's penalty shootout blueprint at the World Cup.

news.headline: Bigger than Rumble in the Jungle? DR Congo bid for World Cup history
news.desc: DR Congo's run at the 2026 World Cup comes 52 years on from both their disastrous debut at the finals and one of sport's greatest occasions held in Kinshasa.

news.headline: England fans' behaviour 'excellent' at World Cup
news.desc: England supporters in the United States are praised for their "excellent" behaviour during the group stage of the World Cup, but domestic incidents are up on recent tournaments.

news.headline: Pick Mainoo over Anderson against DR Congo - Rooney
news.desc: Former England captain Wayne Rooney says he would start Manchester United midfielder Kobbie Mainoo instead of Elliot Anderson in Wednesday's World Cup last-32 tie with DR Congo.

news.headline: The Paraguay hero who sold his kit when his son was born
news.desc: Orlando Gill has become Paraguay's World Cup hero but was in football obscurity 18 months ago and had to sell his kit to support his family after the premature birth of his son.

news.headline: Germany fans in need of hope as prospect of Klopp looms
news.desc: As Germany again fail to progress at a World Cup BBC Sport looks at the fallout from their defeat by Paraguay and what happens next.

news.headline: Ecuador make Mexico fans hotel noise complaint
news.desc: Ecuador make a complaint to World Cup organisers over noisy local fans outside their team hotel before their last-32 match against co-hosts Mexico.

news.headline: Where to watch the World Cup on the BBC and ITV
news.desc: All the TV and radio details for each of the 2026 World Cup knockout games.
